rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS WALKER et al VS. PLATINUM CAPITAL GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief, From Default For Order Extending Time To File Designation Of Experts And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October. 19, 2012. Line 8 - PLAINTIFF LARRY LOYD and NICHOLAS WALKER's Motion For Relief, From Default For Order Extending Time To File Designation Of Experts And For Sanctions. Hearing required. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: LAUREL TIMMS et al VS. YING Y. WANG AKA THERESA WANG, TRUSTEE OF THE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Production Of Business Records On Non-Party Hanover And Awarding Monetary Sanctions; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October. 19, 2012. Line 7 - PLAINTIFF LAUREL TIMMS and MATT MCNALLY's Motion For An Order Compelling Production Of Business Records On Non-Party Hanover And Awarding Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff's Motion fro Order Compelling Production of Business Records is granted. Hanover Insurance Company shall serve all responsive documents on Plaintiff's counsel on or before October 22, 2012 by personal service. Sanctions are granted in the amount of $2,976 to be paid on or before November 17, 2012. Non-party Hanover Insurance failed to cite any authority finding service of subpoenas on a registered service agent improper/insufficient. Cal. Practice Guide cited is not persuasive and not binding. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: LEOPOLDO CAP VS. AMERICAN HOME MORTGAGE SERVICING, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October. 19, 2012. Line 1 - PLAINTIFF LEOPOLDO CAP's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record is Off Calendar. Moving party failed to comply with Court's order 10/15/2012 and SFLR 2.6B. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: 650 EDDY L.P., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. FOUR SEASONS CAFE & RESTAURANT et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October. 19, 2012. Line 2 - DEFENDANT MIKE PHAM's Motion To Continue Trial. Defendant's Motion to Continue Trial is GRANTED. Trial is continued to October 29, 2012. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CINDY OILAR, TRUSTEE OF THE SILVIO L. SCOCCA 1996 VS. ANTHONY BICKEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October. 19, 2012. Line 3 - PLAINTIFF CINDY OILAR, TRUSTEE OF THE SILVIO L. SCOCCA 1996's DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Plaintiff's Demurrer to Amended Answer to Complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to 3rd affirmative defense to allege facts re: retaliation; and as to 2nd, 4th, 7th, and15th affirmative defenses as requested in Defendant's opposition. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to 16th affirmative defense (waiver), no opposition. Demurrer is overruled as to 18th affirmative defense for offset. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CINDY OILAR, TRUSTEE OF THE SILVIO L. SCOCCA 1996 VS. ANTHONY BICKEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October. 19, 2012. Line 3 - PLAINTIFF CINDY OILAR, TRUSTEE OF THE SILVIO L. SCOCCA 1996's MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Plaintiff's Motion to Strike Amended Answer to Complaint is moot. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SYD NAJEEB VS. JOSE A. F. LAGOS et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October. 19, 2012. Line 4 - DEFENDANT JOSE LAGOS and YESSENIA BARRIENTOS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants' Motion to Strike is granted with leave to amend 17f. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SYD NAJEEB VS. JOSE A. F. LAGOS et al
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October. 19, 2012. Line 4 - DEFENDANT JOSE LAGOS and YESSENIA BARRIENTOS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants' Demurrer to Complaint is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith that Lease prohibited use of hotplates. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH ANGELESCO VS. JOE WATKINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October. 19, 2012. Line 5 - DEFENDANT JOSEPH WATKINS's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant's Motion to Quash Service of Summons is granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff's current amendment creates impermissible unexplained inconsistency. Owens v. Kings Supermarket (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 379, 384. =(501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: TERRA VISTA ASSOCIATES ASSOCIATES LLC, VS. IRVING WILSON
rulings.Rulings: Unlawful Detainer Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October. 19, 2012. Line 6 - DEFENDANT IRVING WILSON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer is overruled. Defendants failed to cite any California authority for the proposition that non-compliant notice (FDCPA) cannot support an unlawful detainer action in California. =(501AJR)
